prompt: A warm, handcrafted travel scrapbook spread photographed from directly above on a wooden tabletop, showing an open two-page journal with thick deckled paper edges and layered fabric tabs peeking out from the sides. The style is a realistic mixed-media travel diary with watercolor map art, handwritten Chinese titles, taped instant photos, doodles, and soft natural light. On the upper left page, write the date "2024.09.20." in small handwriting and a large black brush-style Chinese title reading 新疆公路旅行手帐. Arrange 5 Polaroid-style instant photos across the spread, each slightly tilted and attached with beige or patterned washi tape: 1 photo at upper left showing two travelers by a blue alpine lake labeled "天山天池"; 1 tall photo near upper center-left showing three people on a green grassland labeled "那拉提草原"; 1 photo at lower left showing a couple by a deep blue lake labeled "赛里木湖"; 1 photo at upper center-right showing two travelers on a long empty road under a bright blue sky labeled "沙漠公路"; 1 photo at lower center showing two figures standing on dunes at sunset labeled "日落沙丘". Add small handwritten bilingual place annotations near the left-page photos: "天山天池 (Tianshan Tianchi)", "赛拉木草原 (Sayram Lake)", "沙漠公路 (Desert Highway)", and "日落沙丘 (Sunset Dunes)" in a casual notebook style. On the right page, paint a loose watercolor map of Xinjiang in beige, pale green, and blue, with a bold winding blue road route crossing the region and exactly 10 red location pins placed along the route. Include map doodles: a small mountain icon cluster near the top, a river and several blue lakes, a tiny compass in the upper right, 2 camel drawings, 2 yurt drawings, and thin gray roads branching around the main blue route. Along the bottom of the right page, place 3 more taped Polaroid photos: 1 at lower left showing people in colorful ethnic clothing labeled "大巴扎"; 1 at lower middle showing hands holding grilled meat skewers labeled "烤肉串"; 1 at lower right showing a couple inside a yurt labeled "蒙古包自拍". Scatter small hand-drawn doodles throughout both pages, including a sun, a tiny car, 2 horse drawings, 4 fish icons, a crescent moon, several stars, small arrows, flames near the food photo and along the lower map area, and tiny decorative swirls. Keep all text in Chinese where shown, with a lively hand-journal aesthetic, realistic paper texture, slight shadows under every photo, and an overall cozy memory-book feeling of a Xinjiang road trip.
